revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Isonokami no Maro]][[es:Isonokami no Maro]][[fr:Isonokami no Maro]][[ja:石上麻呂]][[pt:Isonokami no Maro]][[zh-classical:石上麻呂]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Isonokami no Maro.jpg|birth_date=0640|death_date={{death date|0717|4|22}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|kanjin|qid=Q11452075|1=kanjin}}|title={{ill|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|1=Chūnagon}}; {{ill|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|1=Dainagon}}; {{ill|Dazai-no-sochi|qid=Q11434484|1=Dazai no Sochi}}; {{ill|Japanese missions to Silla|qid=Q5291074|1=Japanese missions to Silla}}; {{ill|Sadaijin|qid=Q949008|1=Minister of the Left}}; {{ill|Udaijin|qid=Q1351174|1=Minister of the Right}}|children={{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|qid=Q11584755|1=Isonokami no Otomaro}}; {{ill|Q106085619|qid=Q106085619|1=Ishigami no Kunimori}}; {{ill|Q17193139|qid=Q17193139|1=Ishigami no Katsuo}}|relatives={{ill|Q27920460|qid=Q27920460|1=Ishigami no Toyoniwa}}|family={{ill|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}|native_name=石上麻呂}} {{short description|7th and 8th-century Japanese government official}} {{Infobox noble | name = Isonokami no Maro
石上麻呂 | image = Isonokami_no_Maro.jpg | alt = | caption = Illustration by {{ill|Kikuchi Yōsai|en|Kikuchi Yōsai|qid=Q700019|lt=Kikuchi Yōsai|1=Kikuchi Yōsai}}, from ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en|Zenken Kojitsu|qid=Q11397827|lt=Zenken Kojitsu|1=Zenken Kojitsu}}'' | birth_name = | birth_date = 640 | birth_place = | death_date = April 22, 717 | death_place = | other_names = | known_for = }} {{Nihongo|'''Isonokami no Maro'''|石上麻呂||640 - April 22, 717}} was a Japanese statesman of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} and early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}{{cite book|title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to AD 697 |year=2010 |publisher=Routledge |location=London |isbn=9780415594004|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=66aTvdFOqs0C&pg=RA1-PA380|others=Tra.nslated by W.G. Aston|edition=[Reprint]}} His family name was [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe]] no [[Muraji]], later Mononobe no [[Ason]] and Isonokami no Ason. He attained the [[Ritsuryō#Establishment of court Rank|court Rank]] of {{Nihongo||正二位|shō ni-i}} and ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'', and posthumously {{Nihongo||従一位|ju ichi-i}}. In 672 Maro supported Prince Ōtomo (later known as {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|qid=Q357242|lt=Emperor Kōbun|1=Emperor Kōbun}}) in the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} until the prince's suicide. He was forgiven and sent as an envoy to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} in 676. After this he served as a {{Nihongo|judge|法官}}, and as head of the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} in 700. He became centrally involved in politics with a promotion to ''{{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}'' in 701, making ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' in 704 and ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'' in 708. Between 715 and his death in 717 Maro was the most powerful man in the ''[[daijō-kan]]''. Maro is also thought to be the model of {{Nihongo|Isonokami no Marotari|石上まろたり}}, one of Princess Kaguya's five noble suitors in ''{{ill|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|en|The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|qid=Q923593|lt=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter|1=The Tale of the Bamboo Cutter}}''. == Rise to power == Mononobe no Maro first appears in historical documents at the conclusion of the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} of 672, on the side of {{ill|Emperor Kōbun|en|Emperor Kōbun|qid=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}}. His activities in the war are not known, but Maro, along with a few other retainers, followed the Prince until his suicide. In 676, Maro was sent to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} as an ambassador. Japan and Silla exchanged frequent envoys at this time. Maro returned some four months later. Why {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} granted Maro such a position after his side's defeat is uncertain. It may be that his loyalty in serving his lord until the end won him some respect. Alternately, the meritorious service of {{ill|Enoi no Okimi|ja|3=朴井雄君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoi no Okimi|1=Enoi No Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11521581}}, also of the Mononobe family, on Tenmu's side may have softened his family's treatment. Four months after Maro's return, Okimi died and was posthumously named the head of the family. With the 684 reform of the ''[[kabane]]'' system, the Mononobe clan's ''kabane'' was changed from [[Muraji]] to [[Ason]]. The clan's name appears to have been changed to Isonokami around this time.{{cite book |last=Kimoto |first=Yoshinobu |year=2001 |title=律令貴族と政争 |trans-title=Ritsuryō Nobles and Wars |language=Japanese |publisher={{Nihongo|Hanawa sensho|塙書房}} |pages=10–12}} At the funeral service of Emperor Tenmu, Maro spoke a condolence message as a representative of the ministry of justice. In 689, Maro was dispatched with {{Nihongo||石川虫名|Ishikawa no Mushina}} to {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} to deliver court Rank diplomas. He participated in {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}'s enthronement ceremony in 690, and in 700 was put in charge of the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}. In 701, Maro was promoted to {{Nihongo||正三位|shō san-mi}} and from ''{{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}}'' to ''{{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}'' under the new {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}.{{cite book |title=[[Nihon Shoki]]}} Later that year, {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|3=多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}} died, and Maro went with {{ill|Prince Osakabe|en|Prince Osakabe|qid=Q3403344|lt=Prince Osakabe|1=Prince Osakabe}} to deliver a gift from the Emperor to his house. When the ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' {{ill|Abe no Miushi|ja|3=阿倍御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Miushi|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657245}} died in 703, Maro was again the deliverer of gifts and condolences. == As daijin == In 704, Maro possessed the Rank of {{Nihongo||従二位|ju ni-i}}, and was promoted to ''udaijin''. He was now the second highest-Ranking official after the {{Nihongo||知太政官事|{{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|qid=Q1157586|lt=chi-daijō-kanji|1=Daijō-daijin}}}}. Prince Osakabe, and the highest-Ranking official outside of the imperial family. In 705, Osakabe was replaced as ''chi-daijō-kanji'' by {{ill|Prince Hozumi|en|Prince Hozumi|qid=Q3403330|lt=Prince Hozumi|1=Prince Hozumi}}. In 708, Maro was granted the Rank of {{Nihongo||正二位|shō ni-i}}, along with {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|qid=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}. Two months later, Maro was promoted to the long-vacant position of ''[[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]]'', and Fuhito filled his vacancy as ''udaijin''. However, Fuhito is supposed to have been the real political power at this time. In 710, the capital was moved to [[Heijō-kyō]], and Maro was put in charge of the old capital. Four months later, his servant {{Nihongo|Musa no Saga|牟佐相摸}} offered to the Emperor an auspicious melon, and officials both civil and military reported to the Emperor their congratulations. In 715, Prince Hozumi died, leaving Maro as the highest-Ranking retainer. On April 22, 717, Maro died at the age of 78. {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} lamented his loss deeply, sending {{ill|Prince Naga|en|Prince Naga|qid=Q3403340|lt=Prince Naga|1=Prince Naga}} and {{Nihongo|Tajihi no Miyakemaro|多治比三宅麻呂}} on a condolence call to his home and granting him the posthumous Rank of {{Nihongo||従一位|[[List of Japanese court Ranks, positions and hereditary titles|ju ichi-i]]}}. Condolences were presented by representatives of the ''[[daijō-kan]]'', nobles above the fifth Rank, and nobles below the sixth Rank. The [[Shoku Nihongi]] records that {{Nihongo|there was none among the people who did not mourn his loss|追慕し痛惜しない百姓はなかった}}. Eight months later additional presents were made to him, of rough silk, thread, cotton, and cloth. == Genealogy == *Father: {{Nihongo|Mononobe no Umaro|物部宇麻呂, also 馬古, 宇麻乃}} *Mother: Unknown *Wife: Unknown **Daughter: Main wife of {{ill|Fujiwara no Umakai|en|Fujiwara no Umakai|qid=Q1370638|lt=Fujiwara no Umakai|1=Fujiwara no Umakai}} **Son: {{Nihongo|Isonokami no Azumabito|石上東人}} **Son: {{Nihongo|Isonokami no Katsuo|石上勝男}} **Son: {{Nihongo||石上諸男|Isonokami no Morō}} **Son: {{Nihongo||石上大島|Isonokami no Ōshima}} **Son: {{Nihongo|{{ill|Isonokami no Otomaro|en|Isonokami no Otomaro|qid=Q11584755|lt=Isonokami no Otomaro|1=Isonokami no Otomaro}}|石上乙麻呂||?-750}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == *{{cite journal |last=Ōtsuka |first=Taijirō |title=左大臣物部麻呂と壬申の乱 |trans-title=Sadaijin Mononobe no Maro and the Jinshin War |journal={{Nihongo|Ancient Cultures of East Asia|東アジアの古代文化}} |issue=41 |year=1984 |language=Japanese }} *{{cite journal |last=Ōmi |first=Shōji |title=石上左大臣家をめぐって |trans-title=On the Isonokami Sadaijin Family |journal={{Nihongo|Research on Japanese Culture|日本文化研究}} |issue=7 |year=1984}} *{{cite book |last=Kimoto |first=Yoshinobu |year=2001 |chapter=Isonokami no Maro and Fujiwara no Fuhito|title=律令貴族と政争 |trans-title=Ritsuryō Nobles and Wars |language=Japanese |publisher={{Nihongo|Hanawa sensho|塙書房}} }} {{Isonokami Faith}} [[en:Isonokami no Maro]] {{Commons category|Isonokami no Maro}} [[es:Isonokami no Maro]] [[fr:Isonokami no Maro]] [[ja:石上麻呂]] [[pt:Isonokami no Maro]] [[zh-classical:石上麻呂]] {{wikidata link|Q6086140}} [[Category:640 births]] [[Category:717 deaths]] [[Category:Japanese government officials]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:People of Asuka-period Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
